name: Make sendPushNotification enforce pushEnabled by default across all callers
description: WI-226 follow-up: flip sendPushNotification to enforce pushEnabled by DEFAULT (with explicit bypass for transactional pushes), auditing all ~14 callers
acceptanceCriteria:
1. sendPushNotification suppresses delivery when the recipient's pushEnabled=false UNLESS an explicit `bypassPreferenceCheck: true` is passed (default-enforce; the central helper is no longer permissive by default).
2. All ~14 callers audited and dispositioned: every caller that sets the bypass is a genuinely transactional/compliance push (e.g. consent-revocation regulatory notices) and carries a one-line justification comment; every other caller respects pushEnabled.
3. The three WI-82 opt-in sites (sendStruggleNotification, notifyParentToSubscribe, monthly-report-cron) drop their now-redundant explicit respectPushPreference flag — behaviour unchanged.
4. Caller tests that now require it seed pushEnabled=true; full API suite green with no silently-broken notification flows.
Variants covered (security-tag/A10): (a) no-flag caller -> suppressed on opt-out, (b) bypass caller -> always delivers, (c) ex-opt-in sites -> unchanged after flag removal.
bodyText:
Context
WI-82 / WI-226 fixed the push opt-out bypass via an OPT-IN respectPushPreference flag enabled only at the three finding-named sites (sendStruggleNotification, notifyParentToSubscribe, monthly-report-cron). The DeepSec finding's preferred design is for sendPushNotification to enforce pushEnabled BY DEFAULT (it called the central helper 'the wrong layer'). That was deferred because default-enforce silently changes ~10 other push callers (most in files outside WI-82's scope) and would cascade test changes — too broad for the security PR.
Why
Default-enforce prevents the vuln class for FUTURE callers (a new caller can't forget to respect the opt-out). Opt-in leaves the central helper permissive by default.
Scope
Flip sendPushNotification to enforce pushEnabled unless an explicit bypassPreferenceCheck: true is passed.
Audit all callers (trial-expiry, consent-revocation x4, recall-nudge-send, daily-reminder-send, review-due-send, filing-timed-out-observe, weekly-progress-push, nudge.ts, + the WI-82 three). Set bypass only for genuinely transactional/compliance pushes (e.g. consent-revocation regulatory notices) with a documented reason; everything else respects pushEnabled.
Seed pushEnabled=true in the caller tests that now require it.
Acceptance Criteria
sendPushNotification suppresses when pushEnabled=false unless bypass is set; the three WI-82 opt-in sites can drop their explicit flag once default flips.
Every bypass site carries a one-line justification comment.
Full api suite green (no silently-broken notification flows).
Completion Summary — 2026-06-25
# WI-369 — Make sendPushNotification enforce pushEnabled by default across all callers
**What was done:**
Flipped `sendPushNotification` from opt-in push-preference checking to default-on enforcement, renamed the opt-out flag to the AC-specified `bypassPreferenceCheck`, and audited ALL 14 production callers — dispositioning each as either transactional/regulatory (bypass) or engagement/reminder (respect preference). Closes the compliance regression where the first pass left GDPR + billing notices silently suppressed for push-disabled users.
**What changed:**
`apps/api/src/services/notifications.ts`
- Renamed option `skipPushPreferenceCheck` → `bypassPreferenceCheck` (AC requirement #1, verbatim).
- Push-preference check is ON by default; the gate is `!options?.bypassPreferenceCheck && !(await isPushEnabled(...))`.
- Updated the JSDoc to describe the two legitimate bypass cases: transactional/regulatory notices, and callers that pre-checked pushEnabled.
- `notifyParentToSubscribe` (line ~462): no flag — respects preference.
- `sendStruggleNotification` (line ~645): `{ skipRateLimitLog: true, bypassPreferenceCheck: true }` — it pre-checks `isPushEnabled` (line ~592) before reserving the dedup slot, so the bypass avoids a double DB query.
`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/consent-revocation.ts` — added `{ bypassPreferenceCheck: true }` + justification to all 4 calls (consent_warning, consent_archived, consent_expired x2). GDPR regulatory notices — must always deliver.
`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/trial-expiry.ts` (line ~169) — added `{ bypassPreferenceCheck: true }` + justification. Transactional billing notice (trial_expiry).
`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.ts` (line ~646) — no flag (was `respectPushPreference: true` under the old opt-in; default-enforce keeps the same respect-preference behavior). Engagement digest.
Tests updated for the new 3rd arg / renamed flag:
- `notifications.test.ts` — renamed flag; restructured the WI-369 describe to cover the three AC variants: (a) no-flag suppressed when pushEnabled=false, (b) bypass delivers regardless of pushEnabled=false, (c) ex-opt-in struggle path verified unchanged in the existing `sendStruggleNotification` describe block.
- `consent-revocation.test.ts` — added `{ bypassPreferenceCheck: true }` 3rd matcher to 8 push assertions (the consent notices now bypass).
- `trial-notification-send.test.ts` — added `{ bypassPreferenceCheck: true }` 3rd matcher to 2 push assertions.
- `monthly-report-cron.test.ts` — already 2-arg from the prior session; unchanged this round.
**Caller audit (all 14 production call sites):**
BYPASS (transactional / regulatory — must always deliver):
- consent-revocation.ts x4 (consent_warning, consent_archived, consent_expired child, consent_expired parent) — GDPR
- trial-expiry.ts:169 (trial_expiry) — transactional billing
- notifications.ts:645 (struggle) — bypass because the caller pre-checks pushEnabled itself
RESPECT PREFERENCE (no bypass — CONFIRMED checked, respecting is intended):
- notifications.ts:462 (notifyParentToSubscribe, subscribe_request) — engagement
- daily-reminder-send.ts (daily_reminder) — reminder
- recall-nudge-send.ts (recall_nudge) — nudge
- review-due-send.ts (review_reminder) — reminder
- weekly-progress-push.ts (weekly_progress) — engagement digest
- filing-timed-out-observe.ts (session_filing_failed) — engagement/operational notice, not regulatory
- monthly-report-cron.ts (monthly_report) — engagement digest
- nudge.ts (nudge) — parent→child engagement (already quiet-hours gated)
**Verification:**
- All affected suites GREEN: 13 suites / 332 tests pass (notifications, consent-revocation, trial-expiry, trial-notification-send, nudge, daily/recall/review/weekly/filing/monthly callers).
- TypeScript: `tsc --noEmit -p .worktrees/WI-369/apps/api/tsconfig.json` → zero errors.
- Red-green (AC #5):
  - Variant (a): temporarily disabled the default-enforce gate → the `(a) returns push_disabled ... (no flag — default enforcement)` test failed RED (2 failures incl. the dependent struggle GDPR test); restored → GREEN.
  - Variant (b): temporarily made the gate ignore `bypassPreferenceCheck` → the `(b) delivers regardless of pushEnabled=false when bypassPreferenceCheck=true` test failed RED; restored → GREEN.
  - Variant (c): the existing `sendStruggleNotification` describe exercises the real implementation (no mock of the gate) and confirms the ex-opt-in path still respects preference via its own pre-check — unchanged and GREEN.
**Caveats / Follow-ups:**
- `consent-revocation.test.ts`, `trial-expiry.test.ts`, `trial-notification-send.test.ts`, `notifications.test.ts` carry PRE-EXISTING internal `jest.mock('../../services/notifications')` / `jest.mock('@eduagent/database')` mocks with `gc1-allow` annotations. Legacy GC6 backlog, NOT introduced by this WI. No new internal mocks were added. Per GC6 deferral, burning them down here would balloon a compliance-scoped fix; recorded for backlog (4 files).
- `filing-timed-out-observe.ts` (session_filing_failed) classified as engagement/operational and left respecting preference. If product later rules a "your session didn't save" notice transactional-must-deliver, it moves to bypass — judgment call flagged.
**No-flag-needed disposition note:** the default-enforce flip means a caller passing NO options now respects preference automatically. AC #4 (seed pushEnabled=true in failing tests): no test needed pushEnabled seeding — the only failures were assertion-shape mismatches (new 3rd arg), fixed by updating the matchers, not by changing recipient defaults.
